title: Longest Common Subsequence
pattern: Dynamic Programming â€” DP on Strings (2D)
coreIdea: Find the length of the longest subsequence common to two strings, where characters need not be contiguous but must retain relative order. Build a 2D table comparing prefixes of both strings.
recognitionCues: asks for longest common subsequence, not substring
approach: Create a 2D dp table of size (m+1) x (n+1). If characters match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1. If not, dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]). Answer is dp[m][n].
